definitions: A style of wooden chair with a solid wooden seat into which the chair legs and back spindles are mortised, often with a curved backrest.
A type of chair characterized by its turned legs and spindles, and a shaped seat, originating in England in the 18th century.
examples: She inherited an antique Windsor chair from her grandmother.
The dining room was furnished with a set of six Windsor chairs.
He spent the afternoon refinishing a vintage Windsor chair.
